title: Canonical queue state and additive queue projections are separate evidence layers
memory_summary: The canonical operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json still contains the original M-001 completion and older team progress, while newer teams have committed additive operations/queue-updates records describing later work. These are not equivalent state containers. The canonical queue is the shared coordination baseline; additive queue updates are append-only evidence projections. A recent projection may supersede a historical claim about implementation progress without silently changing the canonical queue or proving runtime completion.

records.title: ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is the canonical shared coordination baseline
records.summary: CURRENT_STATE.json instructs teams to read and update shared state without overwriting other teams. ACTIVE_QUEUE.json is the named shared queue baseline and currently records M-001 as completed while retaining older progress for other teams.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Re-read both cited blobs and verify the coordination rule and queue contents.

records.title: Queue-update files are additive team-owned projections
records.summary: Recent continuity, independent-creative, and Cloudflare runs added team-owned queue-update records as separate commits rather than replacing ACTIVE_QUEUE.json. Their existence is evidence of an asserted team update, not automatic canonical consolidation or proof that every referenced runtime result occurred.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Fetch the cited commits and verify they add queue-update records without modifying the canonical queue.

records.title: A newer queue projection can coexist with an older canonical queue
records.summary: There is no direct contradiction between ACTIVE_QUEUE.json lacking M-005 and a committed M-005 queue projection. They answer different questions: the canonical file reports consolidated coordination state, while the projection reports one team's append-only claimed update. Conflict arises only if a consumer silently treats projections as canonical or ignores them entirely.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Audit consumers of operations/queue-updates and ACTIVE_QUEUE.json. Reject this inference if repository rules define queue projections as automatic canonical mutations.

records.title: Queue consolidation requires an explicit reducer
records.summary: A deterministic reducer should read canonical queue state plus additive team projections, validate ownership and predecessor state, preserve superseded history, and emit a proposed consolidated queue diff. It must never overwrite another team's state silently and must keep implementation, verification, deployment, and peer-execution claims distinct.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Prototype the reducer against current queue projections and reject the proposal if deterministic consolidation cannot preserve ownership, chronology, and claim-state boundaries.

records.title: No repository reducer currently proves queue projections were consolidated
records.summary: The inspected canonical queue predates recent additive projections, and no inspected artifact establishes a deterministic consolidation pass. Therefore later team work is preserved as evidence but not claimed as canonically merged.
records.falsification_or_review_route: Locate a committed reducer, consolidation workflow, or later ACTIVE_QUEUE.json blob that explicitly incorporates the cited projections with provenance.

contradictions_resolved.apparent_contradiction: The canonical queue is old, but recent queue-update files say newer work completed.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: The files occupy different state layers. The canonical queue remains the shared baseline; queue updates are additive evidence projections awaiting explicit consolidation.

contradictions_resolved.apparent_contradiction: A committed queue update proves the underlying deployment, runtime, or peer execution occurred.
contradictions_resolved.resolution: A queue update proves only that the repository accepted that record. Underlying claims still require their own bound evidence.

supersession_notes.reason: CM-0009 established temporal supersession inside one queue item's history. CM-0030 generalizes the distinction between canonical state and additive projections across teams.
Staged handoff closure remains valid; queue consolidation adds a separate state-integration requirement.

next_action: Build a deterministic, read-only queue reducer that validates team ownership and provenance, produces a proposed consolidated diff, and never writes ACTIVE_QUEUE.json without an explicit consolidation decision.
